mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales indicate improving consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of RSAFS monthly changes on 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in the majority of months with rising RSAFS after the COVID trough.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 10 percent signals the beginning of a sustained labor market recovery, reducing precautionary saving and boosting consumption, which lifts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 14.7% in April 2020 and has declined to 11.1% by June 2020
failure_condition: After the trigger fires (UNRATE < 10%), the forward 60-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not statistically significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) compared to periods when UNRATE is above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: When both the unemployment rate is above 10% and the federal funds rate is effectively at zero, the economy is in a liquidity trap with deep slack. Monetary policy is exhausted, creating uncertainty that depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08 in June 2020 indicates ZLB
regime_hint: zero lower bound with high unemployment
failure_condition: In periods satisfying the trigger condition, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on UNRATE*FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) with the expected negative sign.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a moderate increase in the CPI signals demand recovery and reflation, which is positive for equities. This hypothesis applies only to the low-inflation regime currently observed.
failure_condition: Over periods where CPI YoY is below 2%, the Rank IC of CPI monthly changes on 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (IC > 0.03, p < 0.10), or the signal performs differently in out-of-sample low-inflation periods compared to the training period.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment exceeds 140 million, it signals that the labor market has recovered sufficiently from the pandemic shock, restoring consumer confidence and leading to positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS in June 2020 was 137.8 million, still below the February peak of 152.5 million
failure_condition: After the trigger is hit, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based recovery in consumer and residential investment demand, generating momentum that pushes equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS in May 2020 increased from April; HOUST in May 2020 increased from April
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST change indicators in a regression of 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05), or the joint signal yields a lower Sharpe ratio than either signal alone over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO monthly changes on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails in expansions as measured by NBER business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: In the deep recession caused by the pandemic, a positive quarterly GDP growth rate after a negative quarter marks the economic trough and initiates a recovery rally in equities over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth turns positive from negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 in Q1 2020 fell sharply; Q2 2020 advance estimate not yet available at decision date
failure_condition: After positive GDP growth following a negative quarter, the forward 60-day return is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10), or the strategy of buying on the first positive GDP print after a recession yields lower returns than buying at the cyclical low.
